name: full-usage-scenario
description: Complex multi-agent scenario covering domain ownership, messaging, domain shifting, and repeated pump-driven wakeups.

steps.explanation: Frontend asks backend for the API schema before touching the UI table.
steps.subject: Need /users payload schema
steps.body: Provide the response shape so the UI table can be wired.

steps.explanation: Frontend parks until the backend replies so the runner exercises inbox-driven wakeups.
steps.resume_hint: Resume wiring the UI table once backend answers.

steps.explanation: The squire wakes backend to answer, then frontend to resume from the reply.
Backend should not be allowed to claim a frontend-owned UI file without a grant.
Backend requests permission to temporarily cross domains for a shared import update.
steps.subject: Need UI file for API import update
steps.body: Please grant a temporary claim for src/ui/app.ts.

steps.explanation: Backend parks until frontend grants the contested claim.
steps.resume_hint: Use the grant to update the shared import.

steps.explanation: The squire wakes frontend to grant the claim, then backend to act on it.
Research expands its writable scope to include UI copy review, simulating a mid-session domain shift.
steps.desc: Expands into UI copy review after cross-team agreement.

steps.explanation: Research informs frontend that the domain shift happened so all agents have the same mental model.
steps.subject: Research expanding into UI copy review
steps.body: I have shifted domain to include src/ui/** for copy review and text updates.

steps.explanation: Wake any agent with inbox work created by the domain-shift broadcast.
Now create a real parked-task cycle: frontend waits on backend and backend waits on frontend with no natural reply coming.
steps.subject: deadlock seed one
steps.body: This is a seed message for the deadlock test.

steps.explanation: Mirror the seed in the opposite direction so each side can park on the other.
steps.subject: deadlock seed two
steps.body: This is the counterpart seed message for the deadlock test.

steps.explanation: Frontend parks on backend's seed message so it appears blocked on backend.
steps.resume_hint: Break the cycle with a documented assumption if designated.

steps.explanation: Backend parks on frontend's seed message so it appears blocked on frontend.
steps.resume_hint: Break the cycle with a documented assumption if designated.

steps.explanation: With no inbox-first progress left, the squire should detect the wait-cycle and wake the designated breaker.
The counterpart should wake on the deadlock-broken info message and clear the remaining parked work.
After the deadlock breaker path runs, no parked tasks should remain.
The scenario should end without any residual wait-cycle in the ledger.
